articles.title: Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese firms 'exploiting' U.S. AI models
articles.summary: The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies' exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the

articles.title: Gambling Companies Celebrate Indictments as Pressure Grows
articles.summary: Gambling companies have cited recent federal charges as proof that illegal activity is being rooted out. The companies’ critics say the cases do not address widespread illicit activity.

articles.title: Appeals Court Says Trump’s Ban on Asylum Claims at Border Is Illegal
articles.summary: The ruling could require the Trump administration to begin processing new applications from asylum seekers at the southern border.

articles.title: Vessels seized by the U.S. this week were part of a global 'shadow fleet'
articles.summary: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Elisabeth Braw, senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, about shadow vessels, after the U.S. military's seizure of two Iranian-linked oil tankers.

articles.title: Iran and U.S. Blockades in the Strait of Hormuz: What to Know
articles.summary: The foes are trying to exert control over the narrow passageway. Most ships aren’t moving. Here’s what to know.

articles.title: A real-life Kraken stalked the seas of the late Cretaceous
articles.summary: Researchers discovered evidence of enormous Kraken-like creatures who hunted in the seas some 100 million years ago, competing with large apex predators.

articles.title: Trump Says He Dislikes Prediction Markets. His Family Invests in Them.
articles.summary: The White House has warned staff not to wager on government decisions, but his family’s involvement with these firms undermines the president’s message.

articles.title: What Are Prediction Markets, and Why Are They Causing Controversy?
articles.summary: The indictment of a soldier who bet on the U.S. operation to capture President Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela put renewed focus on a new way to gamble, and a new way to cheat.

articles.title: Todd Blanche Targets Trump’s Enemies Amid Jockeying to Lead DOJ
articles.summary: Mr. Blanche’s actions are meant to demonstrate progress on the president’s priorities, chief among them payback.

articles.title: The Peril of Piloting Ships Through the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Controllers for shipping companies face a daunting decision as they try to free vessels trapped in the Persian Gulf: “Basically you’re sending someone unarmed into war.”

articles.title: How Kevin Warsh Could Shrink the Fed’s Footprint in Financial Markets
articles.summary: President Trump’s nominee to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve wants to overhaul the central bank, including its more than $6 trillion balance sheet.

articles.title: Justice Department drops probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell
articles.summary: The move paves the way for the Senate to confirm Kevin Warsh, the president's nominee to head the central bank.

articles.title: Decades-old, newly restored Smithsonian carousel reopens — to children's delight
articles.summary: The carousel was first desegregated when part of Gwynn Oak Amusement Park outside Baltimore in 1963. It was moved to the National Mall after the park closed.

articles.title: How Pittsburgh — host of this year's NFL draft — became a sports mecca
articles.summary: The Pennsylvania city is hosting the draft for the first time in almost 80 years. Pittsburghers say the city's passionate fanbases and winning teams make the selection a natural fit.

articles.title: Kushner and U.S. envoy Witkoff will head to Pakistan for new Iran talks
articles.summary: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Islamabad, and the White House says Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire. And, Trump eases medical marijuana rules
articles.summary: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks, President Trump says. And, the Trump administration is easing rules on medical marijuana.

articles.title: Thousands of seafarers stranded by ongoing U.S. blockade on Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.

articles.title: 'Self-aware' robots can learn complex tasks by watching humans. Is that a good thing?
articles.summary: Scientists say they've made a key breakthrough that would allow robots to figure out complex tasks on their own, but experts say it raises questions about how much risk comes with letting robots be in

articles.title: Cuban ambassador says U.S. is to blame for island's crisis
articles.summary: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, a senior Cuban ambassador blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.

articles.title: EU approves a $106 billion loan package to help Ukraine after Hungary lifts its veto
articles.summary: The European Union on Thursday approved a $106-billion loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for two years, ending months of political deadlock.

articles.title: French police probe suspected weather device tampering after odd Polymarket bet
articles.summary: The incident is the latest eyebrow-raising bet on Polymarket, as allegations of rigging and manipulation continue to haunt the popular prediction market site.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
